description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two tall cylindrical red pillars supporting a pair of horizontal crossbars, with the uppermost bar gently curved and painted black. The wood grain texture is visible, giving the structure an authentic, natural appearance. The base of each pillar is capped in black, suggesting reinforcement or protection from the elements. The design is minimalist, with no additional ornamentation or inscriptions visible. The open space beneath the crossbars allows for passage, symbolizing the transition from the mundane to the sacred.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with red wooden pillars, black curved crossbar, and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing both red pillars, the curved black crossbar, and the open passage beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the pillars and the depth of the crossbars, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, showing the same structural elements and open passage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the same profile and depth of the crossbars.
